title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Runtime Contract: Jwt Validation Contract
summary: A service invents password hashing, trusts JWT payloads before verification, accepts any algorithm and audience, stores long-lived bearer tokens in logs, and leaves sessions valid after recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo jwt validation contract increment in authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin allowed algorithms and keys; verify signature, type, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, subject, token use, key rotation, and clock policy before claims.
Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Jwt Validation Contract · Theory Jwt Validation Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2654 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
